Clear indication of CME in AIA 171 and 193: opening/rising field lines starting around 16:30Z, followed by dimmings, two-ribbon flare, outflows.  The CME was fast and bright, and also had a 360-degree halo/asymmetric shock structure.

CME associated with a big filament eruption below the CH south of AR 2371.  The eruption seems to be pushed to low latitudes.  It is a partial halo and very difficult to measure since the front is very faint in the available imagery.
Source could not be identified.  It is possible backsided.  CME was hard to measure due to the available imagery.
Appears to be backsided, no source was seen on disk.
This eruption is part of a continuous outflow from the SE. The outflow can be seen in SDO AIA 171. The exact source location cannot be determined because the eruption appears backsided.
Observed in the center of the Earth-facing disk in SDO AIA 171, 193, and 131 from AR 2371 around 2015-06-21T01:41Z. This eruption is associated with a couple of M-class flares from the same region.
Eruption that merged out of the outflows from the SW quadrant of SOHO. A possible source is faintly visible in SDO AIA 304 around 08:12UT.
Associated with an M6.6 flare. Assymetric halo in SOHO coronographs.
Rising loops and small eruption can be seen in SDO AIA 193 and 211.
Loops can be seen rising in SDO 171, but nothing is visible on the disk.  It may be a back-sided event.
CME associated with a filament eruption seen in SDO 193 north to the AR 2371.

Possibly associated with a small brightening in SDO AIA 193 around 2015-06-23T16:45Z near AR 2371. Although the CME was classifed as C-type, it's narrow and diffusive nature makes it unlikely to have any substantial SWx impacts.
Source location is close to active region 2367 but appears more back-sided.

This CME is associated with an M7.9 class flare from AR 12371.
SDO 193 shows dimmings and a fast EUV wave to the NE and a post-flare loop arcade, CME is a partial halo in C2/3.
So far this event looks backsided, with some rising loops in SDO 171 in the northwest at 13:42UT and in the southwest at 14:00UT. It is very faint.
High Latitude filament / prominence eruption seen in SDO 304 around 10:00UT
Most likely back-sided

eruption behind the W limb, probably in or close to former AR 12371, AIA 193 shows opening of field lines off the west limb starting ~14:26, AIA 304 show additional cool material being eject starting ~14:39, proton fluxes in ACE start increasing ~16:45 (but remained below threshold), SOHO/COSTEP protons also increased.
Large filament eruption seen in AIA 304 off limb in the NE, loops opening in AIA 193, starting ~17:25, source behind E limb (but close to limb, assume lon~-110-120)
Large filament liftoff easily visible in 193 and 304 starting 2015-07-04T19:10Z

Very narrow, faint, and likely a backsided event.

May be from faint opening field lines seen in SDO 171 behind the East limb around 06:30.

CME associated with a small eruption seen on the West limb from AR 2377 around 13:00Z after a B9.4 class flare.  CME is very faint and difficult to measure.  Only POS measurements could be done using STEREO_Cat.
Very faint East limb CME.  POS measurements done with STEREO_Cat.
Associated with rising loops visible behind the SE limb in 193 imagery starting around 14:30Z
No clear EUV source, possibly far-sided.
Likely associated with an eruption observed in SDO 304 from AR 2381 around 2015-07-07T13:30Z.

The source region could be the filament that lifts off around N15W20 in SDO AIA 304 just after 08:25UT. The eruption along with post eruption arcades are also visible around the same time in SDO AIA 171 and 193.  It seems to be pushed more west due to the nearby coronal hole.
It is difficult to find a source location on the disk , but there is a small eruption that can in seen in SDO AIA 193/171 in the southeast around 00:19Z.

filament eruption close the south pole, filament starts to slowly rise at ~ 14.00 UT (seen in AIA 304), C2 streamer to the SE also start to slowly rise, fast rise phase in AIA starts around 20:00, C2 starts fast rise ~22.36 (which was chosen as start time of CME). The filament is very exended in longitude spanning probably half the sun, faint dimming off the SE limb (in AIA 193) seems to be connected with it, implying that the eastern end of the fil is behind the limb. The western part of the fil (in 304) rises but mostly drains away to the west limb. Post-eruptive loops appear in AIA 193, sequentially from E limb to the west. CME in C3 is very asymmetric, mostly the eastern part was fit.
Appears to be associated with a filament eruption from the NW limb
Associated eruption observed off of the NW limb in SDO AIA 304 starting around 2015-07-11T03:07Z.

Narrow and difficult to see in C3
Unable to find a source in SDO 193/171/304

Faint CME, looks like a single wide loop off the NE in C2/C3.
Around 17:30 - 18:00, some change in the field lines in the NE as seen in SDO 171 is visible. This may be from a behind limb source
Rising/opening loops visible starting ~22:00 off limb in the NW, especially in AIA 171.
A prominence eruption from a small unnumbered active region starting at 08:51Z.  Small 2-ribbon flare and dimming.
CME began as rising loops above AR 2381 in the NW starting around ~6:00, most visible in AIA 171 Å.

Visible in AIA 171 as rising loops off the SE limb starting ~09:00Z.  CME is likely associated with the southern filament channel on the far side of the Sun.  This CME may be part of another DONKI CME entry: 2015-07-14T12:00Z.

Visible in AIA 171 images as rising loops above the south pole starting ~ 09:00Z.  CME is likely associated with the southern filament channel on the far side of the Sun.  May be the same CME as the 2015-07-14T10:00Z CME, just two separate loop systems.

Source of the CME is a filament eruption starting at ~14:20 around N30E30 in SDO 193 and 304, very nice eruption in 304, rotation of filament, AIA 193 shows dimmings and wave to the north. second filament eruption was triggered, seen in AIA 304 at ~21.30 (second filament located north of the first one). CME in C2/3 is very faint and straight to the north.
faint signs of eruption in AR 12388 at ~05:00 in AIA 304.
Associated with a small eruption near the eastern limb as seen in SDO AIA 304 around 2015-07-18T14:48Z.
Associated with rising loops observed in SDO AIA 193 around 2015-07-18T22:00Z.
Associated with a filament eruption observed in SDO AIA 304 around 2015-07-18T02:27Z.
Possibly associated with a small eruption around 2015-07-18T09:51Z off of western limb in SDO AIA 304.
Associated with an eruption/opening field lines around 2015-07-18T14:36Z as seen off of the western limb in SDO AIA 193.
Source could not be found. Likely associated with a back-sided eruption behind the western limb.
Far sided and high northern latitude. Most images are from C2 and C3, but STA has 2 images of the CME.
This CME was backsided but close to the northwestern limb as seen as rising loops in SDO AIA 171 around 03:00Z. Rising loops could also be seen on the northeastern limb from STEREO A.
Associated with a small eruption on SW limb in SDO around 2015-07-19T04:37Z near AR 2384.
Associated with large eruption from SW limb in SDO AIA 193/304 around 2015-07-19T07:31Z.
This CME is very faint and small. It is associated with a filament eruption in the northwest around 07:00Z.
This CME is associated with a filament eruption around 07:00Z that was caused by the same activity that caused the filament eruption and CME in the northwest at 2015-07-20T08:48Z.
Believed to be associated with a small eruption visible in STA EUVI 195 starting 12:30Z. CME was very faint
Eruption can be seen faintly behind the NW limb in SDO AIA 193 around 2015-07-21T17:30Z and also in STEREO A EUVI 195 around 2015-07-21T17:12Z.

This CME is extremely dim. A source candidate is rising loops and a flare. Rising loops can be seen in STEREO A EUVI at 2015-07-21T23:00Z in the southeast and a flare can be seen at 2015-07-21T07:30Z at the same location.
CME source is not clear.  It appears to be coming from a backsided AR seen in STA EUVI around N30W35 looking at STA images.
The source of this narrow streamer CME is a small prominence near the SE limb visible in SDO AIA 304, 193, and 171 just after 11:12 UT.

Backsided large dimming area seen in STA EUVI around 16:15Z.  CME is faint and slow and hardly seen in C3 imagery.

CME can also be seen in STA coronagraph imagery, but not used in analysis.
The eruption can be seen quite clearly off the NW limb in SDO AIA 304 beginning around 13:50 UT.
Backsided. The CME source can be seen as a filament eruption in STEREO A EUVI imagery around 17:40 off the NW limb. The eruption can also be seen in the NE of SDO AIA 304 imagery.

May be associated with a small brightening seen in STA EUVI around 0800, but unsure...
No candidate found in SDO
The source location is unknown. Potentially some activity in the Northern region of SDO AIA 304 around 10 UT.
Most likely back-sided since no eruptions/activity seen on the solar disk. Source location not found in STA EUVI either (there is a data gap preceding the start of CME though).
source of CME is far-sided, most likely AR to the NW in EUVI A, shows signs of eruption after data gap between 11.10 and 22.10, AR lon ~-135 deg - this is used for CME longitude
The source is an eruption in the southwest in STA EUVI around 00:10UT.
Associated with an eruption observed in SDO AIA 193/304 around 2015-07-31T15:44Z near AR 2392.
Associated with an eruption observed in SDO AIA 193/304 around 2015-07-31T16:58Z off of the eastern limb and in STEREO A EUVI around 2015-07-31T16:55Z.
Derived parameters are very approximate. No source for this faint/narrow CME could be found.
Associated with a filament liftoff or explosion starting around 18:10Z
Faint, Small CME. Source unknown.  Becomes very faint very soon after it is seen in C2.

In STA EUVI, between the 20:15 and 21:45 frames, an eruption can be seen in the SW Limb.
Same source also seen in SDO 193 and 171 off the SE limb.

CME source can be seen in SDO AIA 193, 171, and 304 as a large filament eruption  on the south east limb.  It can be seen in STA EUVI as a large filament eruption at around S40E120 with some post eruption arcades.
There is no obvious source. It may be backsided but there is no sign in the STEREO images. It may be a part of a streamer.
Can be seen as a small filament eruption in SDO AIA 304 just above AR 2394 and AR 2393 starting at around 14:18 UTC.  SDO 193 shows a distinct dimming and possible EUV wave moving northward.
Associated with a filament eruption in the northwest around 06:06UT in SDO AIA 193. The southern end of the filament is associated with AR2363.

CME measurements are very rough since it was very faint and source could not be clearly identified due to a data gap in STA imagery.  It is assumed to be originated on the far side close to an AR on the NE imagery of STA.
Associated with a large eruption observed on the SE quadrant of the Earth-facing disk around 2015-08-07T20:00Z in SDO AIA 193/304.
Associated with an eruption observed on the SE quadrant of the Earth-facing disk around 2015-08-07T23:42Z in SDO AIA 193/304.

Likely associated with a back-sided eruption that could not be seen in EUV imagery because of data gaps. The source location is approximate. There is a dimming and opening of field lines seen in SDO AIA 193 after 02:00 which would suggest a lower longitude (85 degrees or so).
Associated with rising loops seen on the eastern limb in SDO AIA 304 around 2015-08-08T08:00Z.

Associated with a small filament eruption was observed in SDO AIA 304 around 2015-08-08T23:09Z. This small eruption could not be measured with SWPC_Cat because of its very diffusive/faint nature and the derived parameters were approximated using StereoCAT.
Unable to locate source. However, the CME is extremely faint and slow it is not expected to be geoeffective.
The source appears to be a large filament eruption that can be seen off the NW limb in SDO AIA 193/304 around 11 UT.

Very Faint CME in the NW. not seen in C3... and disappears in C2 shortly after

Maybe related to eruption seen near AR 2394 around 11UT

CME signature is very strange.  It looks like a high density bulge of plasma at the Sun's corona visible from STA EUVI.  In SDO 193/171 you could see the changes on the magnetic field lines behind the limb.
CME associated with prominence eruption visible in SDO 193/171/304 starting around 13:42Z.
CME associated with a second filament eruption near the equator starting around 00UT.  It is visible in SDO imagery.  However, the CME is very faint and difficult to measure.
CME source can be seen on the east limb in STA EUVI  and also at the back west limb in SDO 193/171 as the magnetic field lines opening.
filament eruption, below  AR 2399
Dimming (SDO 193) and rising loops (SDO 171) seen in the NW around 12:15-13:00
Very small puff that could not be analyzed because of its faint/diffusive nature. It is likely negligible.
No source could be found for this faint CME in any of the available EUV imagery. It is likely a back-sided event.

The source appears to be caused by a change in the NW active region at the northern edge of the coronal hole seen in STEREO A EUVI imagery between the data gap at 05:25-09:55 UT. This CME is probably the source of the increase in particle fluxes in STA starting around 10:00 UT.
The source is unknown, however it could be far-sided.
CME source may be at the far side on the East side of STA EUVI images.  It is difficult to be certain due to a data gap in the images.  The CME is very slow and very faint.
CME is associated with a far side region located at N35W36 from STA imagery where some dimming is visible after a data gap in the images.  CME is fast but very high latitude.
preliminary measurement very close to the sun using SWPC_cat & C2/3 and STA COR2, should be verified with backfilled C3 images!
Large dimming area and eruption seen post M1.2 Class flare.  CME is a halo and faint in the coronagraphs.
Small brightening / eruption seen in SDO 193/171/304 around 04:15
Slow looking filament/prominence eruption with opening field lines seen in the NE limb of SDO 193/171/304 starting around 09:00 and continues for a few hours
CME MAY be associated with a very slow eruption visible on the NE limb in SDO 193/171.  Only POS measurements could be done due to the available imagery.

Small eruption (opening field lines, small prominence) seen in SW limb of SDO 193, 304, and 171 around 01:45.

From SWPC: CME was likely related to a coronal disturbance as
seen in SDO/AIA-171 imagery in the vicinity of old Region 2401 (S11,
L=275) and is off the Sun-Earth line.


Likely not: (CME MAY be associated with M5.6 class flare.  The CME is very faint and slow.  No other source could be found.)
source is far-sided seen in EUVI A to the NE
The measurement is of the outer edge of the CME. The inner portion was measured to be too slow. The source is believed to be from an eruption in AR 2403.
New flux emergence in central part of AR 2304 starting ~ 12.00 seen in images, largely confined event, a narrow jetlike outflow is seen in C2/3 to the SW starting ~16.00

Eruption starting from AR 12403 with extensive dimmings to the north of the AR, and activation of the filament in the northern hemisphere. CME in C2 very faint to the west. Note that AR is located at good location to be connected to Earth, at around S14W50

Filament eruption must have happened between 17:15 on Aug 27 and 03:45 on Aug 28 (EUVI STA data gap) and is indicated by high rising loops along a narrow elongated stretch in the SW of the disk in EUVI STA.
Faint CME. Probably associated with a C4.5-class flare from AR2403. No eruption seen in AIA 193 (probably because of a data gap).
Very faint, high-latitude CME as a result of a faint eruption just North of the disc.

CME was very faint in LASCO C3 images.  Measurements are very rough estimates using SWPC_Cat.  Source region could not be identified with complete certainty but it is believed to be originating from AR 2403.
CME appears to be from the far side.  Rising loops can be observed on the NW limb in SDO 193/171 around 10UT.  CME spreads out and becomes very faint in the LASCO images.
Erupting filament observed in AIA304 just behind the east limb at 23:27
A possible source is dimming seen around S12W12 around 18:00 in SDO 193. It is faint in the coronagraphs.
no source could be identified, probably slow streamer blowout with source close to the west limb, starting time is approximate as the C2 bright structure very slowly starts to expand.

source location unknown, probably close to SW limb: some opening and closing field lines seen in AIA 171. A filament seen lifting off in AIA 304, however it is very wide (along latitude line) so again source location is not clear. A slow streamer blowout, therefore starting time not well defined (slow rise starts before 11.24)
The source is hard to see. It can be seen as a faint darkening movement followed by small post eruption arcades in the southwest around 10UT.

Source location is a filament eruption that starts around 2015-09-04T15:30Z and ends at around 2015-09-04T19:00Z and can be seen clearly in SDO 193, 171, and 304 around S10E30.
There is distinct EUV dimming, and post eruptive arcades. The eruption seems to push the material in the eastern direction.
Faint CME potentially associated with one of (or both) eruptions seen in AIA 193 at 2015-09-06T00:52Z and around 04:00.
Very faint but wide CME. The source location is guessed to be significant darkening at ~S15E25 in the early hours of 2015-09-07. The darkening has increased the size of the CH.

Very faint CME with no clear front,  probably associated with the eruption of a long filament stretched along longit. line erupting in the  NW after 10:00. A  dimming observed in AIA 193/171 and faint high posteruptive loops seen in 193. Hard to determine source location because of the length of erupting filament.
There was a CME and prominence liftoff above the NW limb starting around 5:00 UT, most easily visible in AIA 171.
Rising loops from behind the NE limb starting around 10:00 UT in AIA 171.
source is an unnumbered AR at west limb, off-limb opening of fieldlines can be seen in AIA at ~05:00 UT

Source is likely a large filament eruption after 12:00 around N25W10 visible in SDO AIA 193 and 304, or possibly the small eruption off the west rim of the disk seen in AIA 193 and EUVI STA starting around 13:00.

CME is very high latitude.  Opening field lines and rising material and filament lift off can be seen in SDO AIA 171 and 304 at around 15:00-16:00.  The CME might be more back sided than the measurement suggests.
CME is very narrow.  Eruption can be seen on the SE limb in SDO 190/171 around 09:20Z.

CME may be associated with far side eruption visible in STA EUVI around 11UT.  CME was very difficult to measure.  Only POS measurements could be performed with STEREO_Cat. Please remeasure when possible.
Faint partial halo CME associated with the C2.6 long duration flare from AR2415.
Source region never identified, all measurements preliminary
Visible in the SW of C2, quite faint, source unknown

Visible in the NE of C2, narrow and likely back sided with some movement visible in the field lines in SDO AIA 171 just after 14:30.  Cannot be sure as there is a gap in STA EUVI imagery at this time.
This CME is very faint and can be seen in C2. A source has not been identified; it is probably backsided.
This is associated with a series of small eruptions off the NE limb in SDO AIA imagery, starting around 2015-09-18T23:00Z. It is likely associated with a farsided AR visible in the STA EUVI imagery.
No source location identified; likely backsided. Can be seen in the southwest in C2. It is faint but has prominent loops. Needs more images to measure.
Source location is unclear in SDO AIA imagery and it is believed to be back sided. CME is faint and low latitude.

CME parameters were determined using both SWPC and STEREO_cat data. Averaged parameters obtained measuring SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2, and SOHO LASCO C3 imagery. Unsure of source location, could potentially (but unlikely) be from continuous activity off of the East limb seen in SDO AIA 193 and 304.
The source is associated with the earlier M2.1 class flare.
Eruption can be visible in SDO 193/171 from below AR starting at 05UT after a C8.8 class flare (very impulsive).
Most likely back-sided
CME is very thin and fast moving.  Its source is a filament eruption that is just beyond the western limb that can be seen clearly in SDO AIA 304 and 171.
CME is believed to be far sided and caused by a filament eruption that can be seen at 2015-09-22T03:30Z in STA EUVI 195 in the SE.  The CME is very faint and slow moving.
CME is a partial halo that can be seen spanning the entire southern pole in C2.  It is very faint and slow moving.  Its source location is believed to be far sided, but it is unclear.

CME source is unknown. It is believed to come from a similar far side region as the partial halo of 2015-09-22.  Longitude should not be taken as accurate.
Update Karin: EUVI A images show indications of a large filament eruption during the data gap between 2015-09-22T10:35Z and 2015-09-23T10:35Z, a large post-eruptive loop arcade south of the disk center CH, later images (after 2015-09-23T21:20Z) do not show a filament channel anymore that was present 2015-09-22. The CME will probably interact with HSS from the large disk center CH.

filament eruption seen in AIA 193/304 starting ~17.00, filament located east of AR 12422, C3.2 flare in AR 12422 (west of filament but close to it) takes place AFTER filament lift off (flare peak 18:57). AR 12422 has shown considerably flux emergence over the last 2 days which probably triggered the filament eruption.

This relatively faint CME most probably has the same source as the CME 2015-09-23T19:00Z. There is a data gap in AIA 193 at the time of eruption but we can see some new dimming to the NW off AR 12422 after the gap.
Source likely large eruption just after 14:20 from AR 2422 visible in SDO AIA 193, 304 and 171. There was a significant amount of dimming and appears to be moving E.  The CME is narrow and faint.
The CME is believed to be a result of an earlier eruption in the NE that occurred around 00:13 UT.
Large filament liftoff visible in 193 and 304 starting around 2015-09-27T14:40Z
CME associated with M3.6 class flare from AR 2423.  CME is very faint.  Eruption can be seen in SDO 193/171/304.
No clear source, some faint off-limb evolution in AIA 171 images around 16:15 UT.
Source appears to be a broad filament channel in the north; signature is faint, and CME appears to be narrow, so it's not certain.

CME is very slow moving and high latitude.  The source location is believed to be the gradual dimming that can be seen in SDO AIA 193 from 2015-09-29T15:00-24:00, in the upper half of the earth facing disk. The material can be seen moving in SOHO Lasco C2 as early as 17:06UT as the material continues to propagate out very slowly.

CME may be backsided from AR visible EUVI STA east limb.  Longitude is roughly estimated due to the lack of imagery.
update Karin: AIA 193 shows opening of field lines off the west limb starting ~07:10, CME source clearly behind limb

narrow, fast ejection to the west in C2, AIA 193 shows opening of field lines off the west limb at ~13:00,
therefore source location behind west limb
The source location for the CME is now backsided. The location is N05 W90-100.
Eruption visible behind west limb around 1700Z
From a filament eruption from the southwest limb
CME possible associated with   an area of dimming visible in STA (there is a data gap so the source is not very accurate).  POS measurements with STEREO_Cat only since CME is very faint.
This is a very slow moving eruption. Source could be in rising loops seen behind E limb in SDO AIA 171 at around 11:00Z.
This is associated with an ejection seen in AIA 304/193 at the east limb starting at ~06:00, source behind the E limb.
Associated with a small eruption visible in 193 around 17:00Z
Tiny CME off the eastern limb. No source location found.
The source location was near the limb but more back sided.  A small, thin, spitting eruption can be seen clearly in AIA 171 coming from beyond the limb.

There was a large prominence eruption in SDO AIA 304, 171, and 193 beyond the northeast limb that lifted off from around 21:00UT-00:00UT. This produced a CME with a high latitude starting at 00:00Z following a datagap in C2, moving at around 500 km/s.
South of AR 2434. very narrow spray like CME. Prominence material seen in AIA 304Å.
CME appears to be backsided looking at the SDO images but source region could not be identified accurately due the lack of images from STA.
CME appears to be backsided.  There is a visible eruption seen in the SDO images behind the NE limb.  However, location could not be verified due to the lack of images from STA.
A rising loop can be seen over the NNW limb in AIA 171 from 18:00-19:00.  There are signs of filament activity in STEREO-A EUVI images, this is the presumed location of the eruption.

Source was an active region complex located ~15 degrees south of the ecliptic behind the Eastern limb.  Eruption was clearly visible in AIA 171 and 304 as an off-limb spray and eruption from 12:45-13:45.
The source is from the flare that occurred around 20:09 UT from AR 2435.
Source is associated with earlier C-class flaring from AR 2435.
Associated with a filament eruption visible in STA EUV imagery starting around 16:00Z in the NW quadrant.

Relatively faint partial halo CME associated with long duration C4.5 flare from AR2434. CME front is irregular (looks like the middle part of it has been slowed down by something), and so it is not easy to analyze.
The source was not identified, but it is most likely a backsided event.

This CME can be seen in C2 starting at 07:48Z.  It slowly propagates into C3 before mostly dissipating.   The source location is a solar flare that can be seen in the STA EUVI 195 at 04:50Z.  You can see distinct dimming and movement towards the direction of the ejecting CME.
Opening field lines can be seen in SDO imagery off the East limb around 19 UT. The source can also be seen in STA EUVI imagery--it is far-sided.
Source is probably the active region visible in STEREO-A EUVI 193 images.  No data at the time of eruption, but it was erupting before and after.
A large CME that was caused by an eruption coming from AR 2438 near the west limb in SDO AIA.
This CME is very narrow and slow.  It was caused by an eruption on the eastern limb at around 21:50Z.
CME is associated with AR just behind the East limb in SDO.
filament eruption in disk in SE quadrant, not close to any AR, start of lift-off ~11.00 seen in AIA 304, nice dimming regions in AIA 193
CME associated with C1.1 flare and eruption following that elevated SEP levels.
CME may be backsided.  It is combined with a front side CME.  Combination may be responsible for the large SEP event detected at GOES and SOHO.  The measurements are very preliminary.
Small filament eruption seen in SDO 193/171/304 starting around 10UT.
Source not determined: a gap in SDO imagery
Source is eruption from AR 2334 starting around 15:25 visible in SDO AIA 193 and 171 with rising loops and significant dimming.
Source is small eruption in the NW in SDO AIA 171 and 193.
No signatures visible in SDO or STA EUVI imagery.  Likely near lat 90.
Source can be seen as the change in the AR close to the limb in EUVI STA imagery. Associated rising loops off of the NE limb in SDO AIA 304.
Source appears to be associated with activity from AR 2442.
CME visble in the SE, but the source could not be completely identified due to the lack of imagery. It has been measured as originating for an AR in the far side.
Appears to be associated with some opening field lines visible behind the SW limb in 193 starting 20:15Z

Faint, rising loops and dimming appearing above the East limb in AIA 171 starting around 09:40, following by a fast, narrow ejection of material around 13:00.  The source appears to be behind the bright AR approaching the East limb (~5 degrees north).
Source is eruption starting at 01:04 visible in SDO AIA 171, 193, and 304 with dimming and opening field lines.
Source is a large filament eruption associated with the M1.9 class flare that peaked at 03:25 visible in SDO AIA 171, 304, and 193.

CME is connected to a M3.7 flare at AR 12443 very close to disk center, GOES light curve is a long duration event, with extensive post-event loops visible in AIA 131. Large, fast wave emanating from flare site, dimming to the west and north of the AR in AIA 193, dark absorption material ejected at west end of AR seen in AIA 304
CME associated with an eruption visible in SDO 193/171 and 304 from behind the SE limb.
Activity seen in AIA 193 and 304 at ~16:40 close to the west limb, AR 12441

CME start time approximate as the CME has no well defined front, there are outflows in the same direction the whole day. At ~20:40 AIA 193 shows dimming of the off-limb corona at the east limb, with post-eruptive loop arcades appearing later indicating an eruption close behind the limb. EUVI images show a large AR close the west limb but no images are available to confirm the eruption.

This CME is very slow and very faint.   It was caused by an eruption that can be seen at 05:28Z in SDO AIA 193 and 171 in the SE coming from AR 2449.  There is significant dimming of the corona as well as movement of field lines.

Eruption from AR 12448/12451 complex starting ~13.00 in AIA. AR 2448 is at N06W09, AR 2451 at N05W26 around the start of the event, source seems filament located between the 2 ARs. No flare in GOES connected with it. Wave from event to the NW seen to push on the northern CH boundary, post-eruptive arcade visible in AIA 193 and footpoint brightening seen in 304. Note that source might be well connected to earth, that CME is only partial halo to the NW

Eruption from 
AR (unnumbered) + filament in the SE starting ~21:00 (AIA 193/304), located ~S30E60, no flare in GOES
Source is currently unknown. No activity visible in SDO data, so CME is assumed to be backsided.
Associated with a large filament eruption visible in 193 and 304 starting 22:00Z south of ARs 2452 and 2453
CME associated with a second filament eruption visible in SDO 193/171/304 starting around 02UT.
Associated with field-line opening behind the NE limb (AIA193), and with prominence acitivity (AIA304) starting 01:54
Extremely faint CME from a localized eruption.
CME associated with a rotating filament liftoff south of disk center. CME has a complex shape (appears very much like two CMEs in coronagraphs) and is difficult to analyze.
Probably part of one CME starting at 09:12 - result of a rotating filament eruption.
Likely corresponds to opening field lines off the SW limb in SDO AIA 193 between about 2015-11-24T21:57 and 2015-11-25T1:03.
Unknown source.
No clear EUV signature - there was a lot of outflow near the ARs in the SW but nothing definite.
Unknown source.
Source is not quite clear.  It could be associated with a disturbed filament seen on the SE liimb in SDO 171.
Source appears to be back-sided. Eruption can be seen in the center of the disc in STA EUVI imagery.
Associated with activity noted behind SW limb in 193 starting ~ 01:45Z.  Post eruption loops can be seen in STA EUVI on the SE (very close to the limb) around 06UT.
Source location could not be accurately identified.  Longitude has been given by using STEREO_Cat with both SOHO and STA images.
AIA:  A C3.6 flare, dimming and also some rising loops off-limb.
Possibly associated with the earlier CME and flare at 2015-12-01T09:54Z.
Source likely to be from an eruption signaled by the rising loops and dimming in the NW around AR 2458  that starts around 2015-12-01T23:00Z.
CME associated with a C4.0 class flare that peaked on 17:10Z from AR 2462.  Eruption is visible in SDO 193/171.
The source of the CME appears to be backsided activity off the NE limb in STA EUVI imagery. Extremely faint CME that can only be seen in SOHO LASCO C2.
The sources appears to be a backsided eruption off the East limb in STA EUVI data
Eruption associated with a C1.2 class flare from an unnumbered active region.  CME measurements are very preliminary due to the data gap on the images.
CME associated with eruption seen in STA EUVI around 14UT close to S20W60.
Source is a a filament eruption in the SE (~S20E40) between AR 2464 and AR 2463.
Eruption can be seen clearly in SDO AIA 193 and 304 as moving and opening field lines.
Source is likely back-sided eruption visible in STA EUVI around 21:45 in the active region at about E60. Appears to be deflected southwards.
An eruption accompanied by brightening can be seen just SE of the disc in SDO AIA 304 around 10:30 UT
Can be seen as a large filament eruption across the eastern side of the disk starting at around 14:30.  The eruption is seen best in SDO AIA 171 and 304.  Is very faint in SOHO Lasco.

Filament eruption from center of AR 12468 close to disk center, dimmings visible to the north and east of the AR - CME has much lower longitude, no full halo, only eastern part of LASCO C3, this eruption most likely triggered a filament eruption a few hours later
(2015-12-16T14:48Z), this very extended filament is SE of the AR, filament spine is north-south oriented and one end seems close the AR 12468.

Source of this CME is a filament SE of AR 12468 which is close to disk center. AR 12468 had a C-class flare and filament eruption earlier (2015-12-16T09:36:00-CME-001 and  2015-12-16T08:34:00-FLR-001), which probably triggered this filament eruption (northern end of the filament seems connected to AR 12468), no dark absorption material can be seen to lift off, neither AIA 304 or 193, but both show very extended post-eruptive loop arcades and foot-point brightenings.

CME caused by a filament eruption that can be seen most clearly in SDO AIA 304 and 171 and also STA EUVI 195.  The lift off occurred just beyond the SDO limb and most of the material returned back to the sun.
Source is far-sided, eruption visible in STA EUVI imagery in the west. Corresponding opening and moving of field lines in the north east of SDO 193.
Source appears to be backsided. No signs of source in AIA or STA data.
Associated with an eruption from AR 2468 starting 19:24Z in 193 imagery.
This CME is associated with some dimming and post eruption loops visible in SDO 193/171 starting around 2015-12-20T21:58Z.
Associated with an M2.8 flare that peaked at 01:03Z.  Measurements are a little rough since CME mixes with a previous slower CME.
Some eruption and activity visible in STA EUVI starting at 05:00Z near S25E70.
CME is very narrow and associated with the M1.1 flare that peaked at 10:19Z.
A C6.2 flare from AR 2473 and some sign of outflow above the limb was observed in AIA 171 images.
A narrow streamer-aligned CME.
Possibly associated with M4.7 flare in the SE or prior activity from the region.
Source location could not be accurately determined.  It could be associated with a far-sided rising loop eruption visible only in SDO 171 starting around 2015-12-22T22:00Z.
Source is filament eruption visible off the SW limb in SDO AIA 193, 171, and 304 around 04:00 traveling N. The source is likely backsided.
Follows a series of southern CMEs in the S of C2 and COR2A.  Probably backsided.

slow rising eruption (later speeds up) from the SW part of AR 12473. A few hours after the M1.1 flare from the same region. CME "associated with a non-impulsive enhancement in X-ray
activity originating from Region 2473."-SWPC forecast discussion
CME is associated with a M1.8 class flare from AR 2473.
Faint CME visible in the SW of C2 and C3. Source is unknown.  Likely an eruption from AR 2473, though no eruptive signatures line up well with this CME.
Very faint. Source may be far sided and correspond to movement in field lines in SDO AIA 171.

CME is caused by an M2.3 class flare peaking at 00:13Z that caused the remaining filaments in the area to lift off resulting in a rather large eruption with lots of moving and opening field lines and post eruptive arcades in SDO 193, 171, and 304.
This is associated with a faint, narrow, almost continuous stream of material visible in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 03:00Z.

Very faint CME seen off the equatorial western limb is most likely back-sided since no eruption on the front facing disk can be seen in the region. Backsided imagery has a large gap from January 1st so source location not identified.
The CME is associated with an eruption of a long filament in the SE, seen in SDO AIA 304 after 10:00 and in AIA 193 after 11:00, with rising post-eruption loops seen in 193 around 13:00.

Source of this CME is a filament eruption seen in EUVI A 195 starting 2016-01-04 22:45 UT close to the E limb, rise of dark filament, wave and dimmings at one end of filament footpoints can be seen (other end too close to limb and obscured), AIA 304 shows some signs of this eruption at the W limb (although rather subtle signature).

CME initiation is very gradual, a slowly expanding streamer.  There is not much of an EUV signature, except perhaps some faint off-limb opening field lines in AIA 171 at around 03:00Z (i.e. after the streamer has already started to expand).
Source is apparently the location of AR 2437 from behind the western limb.  Large filament eruption starting around 03:00 in AIA 304, but most of it appears to drain back.
Appears as a bright loop in the LASCO C2 images. No apparent related activity in EUV imagery.
source of this CME is on the far side, EUVI shows eruption signatures in the NW close to an AR starting ~08:05 UT, AIA 193 shows opening of field lines off limb.
This CME was caused by an small faint eruption that can be seen in SDO 193 at around 15:00Z.
No discernible source in EUVI imagery. This was a small, narrow CME.
CME in the NE of C2 and C3.  Source undetermined.
There was some evidence of a high-latitude filament eruption from 08:00-12:00Z in the EUVI-A 195A images.
Filament eruption visible in EUVI-A ~1200Z near S10E50.
Very faint, only observed in C2. No source region was identified.

filament eruption close to the NE limb seen in AIA 304 (most prominent) and to a lesser extent in AIA 193 starting around 08:30 UT, some signature also seen in EUVI A, some post-eruptive loops seen later rising from behind the solar limb (seen in AIA 193). Initial start of eruption of filament seems to originate close to AR 12484.

The CME is associated with a filament eription seen beyond the east limb in AIA 193 around 20:00. The source location is seen as rising post-eruptive loops in STEREO A EUVI after 21:05 around ~(-100,10) lon/lat.

The source of this high northern latitude CME is not very clear: there is definitely some activity seen in STEREO A EUVI after 00:15 on the north western rim, however it is minor. It is much less clear than the rising loops to the south from there which indicate the source for the CME with Activity ID: 2016-01-12T21:39:00-CME-001 a few hours earlier.
This CME is associated with a filament eruption to the SW starting around 21:45Z.  CME is a partial halo, very faint and difficult to measure.

A very faint and slow moving CME that can be detected in SOHO lasco C2 at around 06:24Z in the northeast.  The source location is likely AR 2483 as it has been releasing small eruptions quite consistently for the last day.
Southward, faint CME seen in C2/C3. There was a gap in C2 imagery from 19:12-00:00 that makes it difficult to give an accurate start time. The source is most likely backsided.
Possible source is opening and closing field lines around 2016-01-16T20:58Z.
A clear eruption in EUVI-A starting at 11:25Z, from an active region located S10E43 in the STEREO-A FOV.  Flare, dimming, and prominence eruption.

Source could not be completely identified.  However, it is visible from SDO AIA 171 that the source comes from the far side.  Measurements and longitude has been calculated using the available images and SWPC_Cat.  Measurements are very rough since CME is very faint.
Possible source is an eruption seen in STA EUVI imagery around 17:55Z in the southeast.
Source is a filament eruption off the SE limb in AIA 193 and 171 beginning around 11:30Z.

Filament eruption seen in AIA 193 at NW limb starting at 11:10 with opening of field lines off limb, erupting structure is mostly behind the limb, post eruptive loops visible behind the limb in AIA 103 and 304.
A small filament eruption was visible in STEREO EUVI-A 195 images starting around 20:00Z.  Location was in the SW of the STEREO-A images.
This CME is associated with a large filament eruption around 17:00Z in the southwest, seen in SDO AIA 193, 304, and 171.
The source for this CME is not completely certain.  It may be associated with a C2.8 class flare that peaked at 07:02Z.  The CME is very narrow and faint.

CME source is a C9.6 flare in AR 12488, seems more like a confined flare, no dimming, waves or filament material connected to flare, CME is very narrow, previous CME (2016-01-28T07:24Z) very similar (narrow, same POS path).

This is a large filament eruption that can be seen starting at around 20:39Z in AIA 193 in the SW, with a nice post-eruptive loop arcade forming afterwards seen right at the limb. Event is connected with a long duration C2.0 flare with flare peak at 21:44. Note, that this eruption might be caused by another earlier filament eruption, starting around 08:00 southeast of the above filament probably connected to the same filament channel.
Associated with a filament eruption and dimming in the east around 14:00Z.
Source is a large filament on the E limb in SDO AIA imagery that started lifting off around 03:30.

Dimming and outflows starting around 05:00UT are visible in AIA images, particularly 193A, coming from the area west of AR 2489 - the active region doesn't show much change compared to the adjacent region.
Dim CME with the front difficult to trace even in difference images. Probably associated with a filament eruption to the east of the disk center starting after 17:30.
A loop and dimming are observed at the western limb in AIA 193 images starting before 15:00Z
possibly from S20E45 in EUVIA
Very slow narrow CME associated with the floating filament eruption from SE seen in AIA 171Å and 304 starting around ~11:50Z; opening of field lines seen in AIA 193 on the east rim at 13:34.

This CME was at a time when a series of multiple dimmings and eruptions can be seen across the disk all occurring at the same time in SDO AIA 193.  There was a filament eruption in the SW that lifted off starting at around 19:00Z that is likely the cause of the CME.  The start time of the CME might be slightly earlier, as there is a data gap in SOHO as it arrived in C2

CME is associated with a filament eruption starting on the farside seen in STA EUVI and connected all the way to the NE seen in SDO 193/171.  The start time of the CME is complicated since it corresponds to a large filament eruption that started very slow.
CME is very faint and narrow.  Measurements are very rough using POS approximation with LASCO C3 images.

Filament eruption starting ~20:30 UT in the NE in AIA 304, part of it is at the NE limb, but it is connected to a long filament going all the way to disk center lying at the polarity inversion line between AR 12497/12496 complex and the rather decayed AR 12492, extensive post-eruptive arcade visible in AIA 193 and footpoint brightenings in AIA 304.
Filament eruption close to the NW limb seen in AIA 304/193 starting ~01:40  - slow liftoff plus dimmings.

Filament eruption close to starting ~04:00 at the northern edge of AR 12491 in AIA 304. The 06:48 eruption appears to be accompanied by some opening field lines in the SW starting around 04:45 in AIA 171, possibly from behind the limb, though there is no indication of activity in EUVI-A.  There may be mixed sources to this CME.
This CME resulted from a filament eruption seen in AIA 193 and 304 at the east limb starting around 16:00Z.
Small CME associated with a small eruption visible in SDO AIA 193 starting from 17:00Z.

C8.9 flare originating from AR 12496/97 complex (with peak at 21:03), eruption from near the polarity inversion line between the 2 ARs, wave going to the north and west, AIA 304 shows dark absorption material being ejected, nice post-eruptive loops in AIA 193, CME is partial halo seen in LASCO and STA.
The source for this CME is most likely backsided.
This CME was very faint in C2 and C3 and was clearly back-sided.
Faint eruption from AR2501 in the NE of AIA 193A images starting at 18:20 - 20:00, a brightening, dimmings and opening loops.
Faint, rising loops can be seen in AIA171 images in the SSW from 06:00-07:30. No STEREO EUVI images all day until 06:15, but there appears to be a dimming in the AR located ~ S15E45.
This is from an eruption that can be seen at around 16:00Z coming form the AR 2497.  The eruption is characterized by rising loops and opening field lines.

filament eruption in the SE quadrant, starting ~22:30 UT seen in AIA 193 and 304, 304 shows untwisting of filament during the rise phase with lots of dark absorption material, post-eruptive loop arcade visible and arcade footpoint brightenings. Event seems to trigger another eruption behind the east limb starting ~ 03:00 (2016-02-19).
This is associated with a backsided filament eruption starting around 13:15 and seen in the NE of STEREO A EUVI 195 imagery.
This is associated with dimming seen in the NE of STEREO A EUVI 195 imagery.
source of this CME is on the far side, can be seen in EUVI A in the SW and in AIA 193 and 171 opening of field lines off limb at SE limb.
This filament eruption can be seen clearly in SDO AIA 193 near the eastern limb.  There is a significant pulse and movement of material as well as opening field lines.
Caused by the eruption on the eastern hemisphere in STA EUVI 195 at 04:55Z. Significant dimming and moving field lines can be observed.
AIA 193 shows opening of field lines off limb in the SW, and lift-off of prominence (starting ~06:30, note that there is an AIA eclipse shortly afterwards), source behind SW limb.
Nice filament eruption in AIA 193 and others, also observed at the west limb as expanding loops and dimming in STEREO-A EUVI
Source is far sided, visible in STA EUVI around 10:45Z near W25N20.  Can be seen lifting off from C2 as early as 09:36Z.
This is associated with activity from a filament eruption on the NW limb in SDO AIA 193, 171, and 304.
Filament eruption in the south-west quadrant seen in SDO between about 2016-03-08T08:00Z - 2016-03-08T17:00Z.
CME may be associated with an AR on the far side seen in STEREOA EUVI.  Eruption begins around 14:30Z.  CME may have a very weak impact on Spitzer around 2016-03-13T11:30Z.

source region at the NW limb in AIA 193 (opening of field lines off limb, small eruption visible at limb AR (probably AR 12512). Note, that there are no C2 images for a few days, identification done by C3. rather narrow, but somewhat fast ejecta, connected with a B 8.4 flare with peak at 18:54.
Source unknown.

The source location for this CME is unclear. It appears to be back sided.  IT can be seen in COR2A as a rapidly growing streamer that ejects.  The start time is actually slightly earlier, but 00:00Z is the first instance of data.
The source location for this is unclear, may be backsided.
Eruption seen in SDO 193/171/304 starting around 19UT.
CME associated with a filament eruption north of AR 2521.  Dimming is visible in SDO 193/304.  It may have a very weak glancing blow at Earth on March 17th.
Filament eruption at SE limb starting ~15:00 (slow rise phase), lift off at ~17:00, seen in AIA 193/171/304.
No apparent source, it was a streamer blowout so not surprising.
Associated with large filament eruption at NW limb around 5:30
Associated with large filament eruption at NW limb around 5:30
filament eruption behind W limb seen in AIA 304 at ~02:40, also seen in EUVI A at E limb, eruption starting ~02:15

eruption close to W limb in AR 12522, connected to C2 flare, very fast wave visible in AIA 193 over a large fraction of the western part of the sun, AIA 304 shows eruption of a filament from the AR (twisted flux rope in emission off limb). Small SEP event connected with this, enhanced proton levels in GOES flux, but below notification threshold.

CME associated with filament eruption visible in STA EUVI at N05E80. Filament can be seen in AIA 304 lifting off the west limb (starting ~02:34). The CME is very faint and only plane of sky measurements could be performed.

CME source could not be accurately identified.  Eruption seems visible behind the west limb in SDO 193/171 starting after 12UT.  The source is almost not visible in STA EUVI images due to the resolution.  Measurements are very preliminary.
CME associated with a filament eruption clearly seen on the western rim in AIA 304 starting at 2016-03-17T21:30. A corresponding opening of field lines is seen in AIA 193.
filament eruption seen in AIA 193/304 at SW limb, opening of field lines off W limb in AIA 193.

filament eruption at SE limb seen in AIA 304/193 starting ~18:45. In addition, a previous eruption from the same location starting ~14:00 produced a slow CME (~200 km/s), this second one is faster and catching up with the first one.
CME is very faint.  Measurements have only been performed using STA imagery.  It is associated with the C1.1 flare that peaked at 03:50Z.
Source location behind limb, can be seen in STA EUVI around N45E40 with some dimming, starting ~16:00. AIA 304 shows rising filament at east limb ~ 14:30
Eruption seen in EUVI A, starting ~09:45 in an AR in the NE quadrant, AR south of CH boundary, located at around lat=15, lon=160
CME is associated with a small eruption visible in the SW in SDO 171/193/304.  CME is very faint.  Measurements are very preliminary.
Source is likely small eruption in SW visible in SDO AIA 171 and 193 with dimming and some opening of field lines.
The source location is unknown. This CME was very faint and narrow
CME associated with a large filament eruption observed on the NE in SDO 193/171/304.  CME is slow but it has a very nice symmetric shape.
The associated eruption was observed by STA EUVI, close to the east limb (as seen from) STA corresponding to roughly W120S25 starting at ~03:55.

It's not clear what the source of this CME is.  There is some evolution in the north of AIA images (particularly 193A) near the coronal hole boundary after 11:00Z, this may be a very faint filament channel eruption (without much filament) or it may have a different source.

filament eruption close to central meridian in the north hemisphere, actual filament lift-off is not visible in AIA, but post-eruptive arcade and double ribbons can be seen in AIA 193 and 304 starting at ~14:00.

The very narrow CME is associated with a very interesting eruption visible in 193/171 starting around 09UT. Twisting of the field lines is visible specifically on SDO 171.  However, the CME is very faint.  Measurements are done using POS approximation.
Eruption from behind the W limb (close at equator) seen in AIA 193 at ~23:00 UT, also seen in EUVI A at the east limb.
Source is likely dimming in STEREO A EUV north-west quadrant starting at about 2016-04-08T10:30Z
This CME is caused by a small eruption that can be seen in the SW of SDO at ~17:45Z.  The CME is very thin.
This CME was seen as opening and closing field lines as well as dimming in SDO AIA 193, from AR 2529 in the NE. It is associated with a long duration C2.8 flare.

filament lifting off seen in AIA 304 at ~10:00 UT (with slow rise phase starting a few hours before that), filament seen to move to the SW. Eruption also faintly seen in AIA 193, only small part of a very long filament channel is erupting (eastern end of the channel).
This CME was associated with opening and closing field lines seen in SDO AIA 193 and 171 around 05:58Z from AR2529 in the NE.
Source is filament eruption near AR2529 around 10:06Z in SDO AIA 193, 171, and 304 with opening field lines visible off the limb of STA EUVI imagery.

This CME may be associated with the AR visible in STA EUVI close to the west limb and the eruption visible in SDO 913/171 on the east limb.  The CME is very faint in STA COR2.  Measurements are very rough.
The CME is a complex partial halo. It is associated with a filament eruption at the back-side which can be observed in AIA 304 over the south limb starting at 2016-04-11T23:37.
Likely source is small eruption see in the NE of STA EUVI 195 at around 17:25.
CME may be associated with activity recorded at an active region seen at the NE on STA EUVI.
Source location is unclear although it is apparent that it is back sided.
Eruption from AR located at the far side seen in STA EUVI starting after 09UT.  Measurements are very rough since the CME is very faint.
Very narrow streamer from far sided AR seen in STA EUVI on the NW.
This CME is associated with a back sided eruption in the north east visible in EUVI 195 around 02:15.
Source is active region in NE of STA EUVI imagery.  Start time is an estimate as the CME began while there was still significant outflow from the previous 2016-04-24T04:24Z CME from the same source.
CME is a full halo from AR on the far side seen from STA EUVI.  Longitude has been taken as the longitude from the spacecraft.
CME is a full halo from AR on the far side seen from STA EUVI.  This CME is faster than the previous one from the same AR.  Longitude has been taken as the longitude from the spacecraft.

source location unknown, nothing visible in neither AIA nor EUVI A.
very narrow, jet-like flow

Small eruption visible at the NW limb in AIA 193 starting ~17:00 (opening of off-limb field lines), source behind the limb, also visible behind the limb in EUVI A (NE limb). CME was small and very narrow.

Former AR 12529 is the source of this CME, EUVI A shows a flare at 04:25 followed by a dimming which moves to the NW of the AR. Although the location of the AR is in the eastern part of the disk (from STA view) the CME in the STA coronagraph is visible in the NW! This CME has a very non-radial path in the lower solar atmosphere. The CME is also visible in LASCO C2/3 to the NE. STA particle data also shows small enhancements at ~05:00.
CME is associated with a B8.1 class flare from the AR 2535.  CME is very faint and measurements are very rough.
CME associated with C1.8 class flare from AR 2535.  CME is very high latitude and combined with previous CME outflows.  Measurements are very rough.
CME is associated with a C1.1 class flare from the AR 2535.  CME is very faint and measurements are very rough.

CME is associated with an eruption visible in the far sided in STA EUVI.  The CME front was very difficult to single out since it is in the same direction (NE as seen from SOHO) as other slow eruptions from AR 2535.
CME is back sided and can be clearly seen in Stereo A EUVI 195 at ~04:00 just west of center disk.
Source location is likely an eruption on the back side around N30W40 in STA EUVI imagery around 17:05. Eruption characterized by dimming.
This CME is associated with an eruption on the back side around N20W35 in STA EUVI imagery around 05:55.

CME is associated with the C3.5 class flare that peaked at 08:42Z.  The eruption was triggered by the flare and it looks like the dimming covers an area southward of the AR as observed in SDO AIA.  The CME front is almost non-visible.  It was measured using STA COR2 images only and measurements are very rough.
This CME was small and faint. Possible source is a small amount of dimming in SDO AIA 193 in the southwest around 18:18.
Faint CME associated with C1.3 class flare from the region 2535 N06W61 at 13:41
This CME was associated with opening and closing field lines off the northwest limb. It is visible in SDO AIA 193 around 13:05Z. The CME is very faint.
The source of this CME is opening and closing field lines off the southwest limb seen in SDO AIA 193 around 14:00.
This CME is associated with a large amount of dimming seen in SDO AIA 193 in the northeast starting at 15:36.
CME associated with filament eruption near N35E20.  However, most of the material looks like traveling to the NW.
The source appears to be a far sided AR located in S10W30 (STEREO A).
Sign of an eruption between ARs 12542 and 12541 in AIA 193 starting at around 00:40, dimmings seen in AIA 193.
A large filament eruption can be seen on the north western limb in SDO 304 starting at around 10:23Z.  The subsequent CME is fast but very narrow and high latitude.
Eruption close to AR 12544 in the NE, starting ~10:00 UT in AIA 193, some dimmings and brightenings visible.
CME is associated with a small filament eruption visible in SDO AIA 304 in the SW.  The start time can be wrong since the outflow started really slow and the CME front is not very well visible.
Source is a filament eruption on the NW starting after 13UT.
Source is likely the filament lift off in NE between 17:40 and 23:36 seen in SDO AIA 193 and 304.  Corresponding rising loops visible in NW of STA EUVI imagery.
The source is likely a far sided filament eruption visible in the SW of STA EUVI Imagery.
This CME is very bright. It is associated with opening field lines as well as dimming in the NW of SDO AIA 193 starting around 15:10.
CME source could not be accurately identified.  Many C and B class flares from ARs on the west side limb (2544, 2545).
No clear source, though there was some off-limb loop evolution north of the equator over the western limb, best visible in AIA 171.
source region is seen in EUVI A close to the equator at around 150 longitude, AR close to east limb east of equatorial CH, eruption starts around 15.00 UT

CME associated with a double peak flare that I believe come from 2 different ARs.  The second peak (13:51UT) corresponds to AR 2546 where a small eruption is visible in SDO imagery.  Measurements are very rough.
CME is very narrow, with the start time an estimate due to a data gap in SOHO before 10:00.  Source is likely material seen erupting off the SW limb in SDO AIA 171 at about 09:18.
Start time is an estimate due to data gap in SOHO. Source is unclear.
Source is likely a small amount of material seen leaving solar disk on SW limb in SDO AIA 171. CME is small and fades in SOHO LASCO C3.
Source is opening and closing field lines seen on the NE limb in SDO AIA 171 as well as STA EUVI imagery.
Source is B6.5 flare accompanied by an eruption from AR 2548 (N15W70) around 08:57.
Most likely back-sided
CME is far sided.  Eruption can be seen in STA EUVI starting around 16:15Z from the cluster of ARs seen on the East side.  The longitude of the source is around 151 degrees.
This CME is far sided and associated with an eruption around longitude 150, and is from the same region as the CME at 2016-05-31T17:09Z.

CME is from the same source as the CME starting at 2016-05-31T17:09Z and the one starting at 2016-05-31T19:09Z.  Far sided source from a cluster of ARs seen on the east in STA EUVI.  Some of the material appears to be going into high southern latitudes as seen in the LASCO data (there is a data gap before the event is visible).
CME is far sided coming from eruption observed on the NE limb in STA EUVI starting around 15UT.  It can also be seen in the SDO 171 images on the NW limb.
Narrow dim CME (a puff) seen in SE in C2. No associated eruption found for it, so POS parameters only.
A dim CME with POS of 300 km/s, somewhat obscured by the handle. The source is unclear.
Possible far sided CME.  Source not accurately identified.  CME is very faint and only POS measurements could be performed.
Start time is an estimate due to a data gap in SOHO LASCO C2. Source is a far sided eruption from 07:30 - 14:00 from an active region in the SW of STA EUVI imagery.
CME is associated with a far sided eruption visible in STA EUVI close to disk center.  Post eruption loops and footprints are visible.
Source region is likely the filament eruption seen in STA EUVI in the southern hemisphere as brightening starting around 18:00 UT.
Source is eruption characterized by opening and closing field lines in the NE of SDO AIA 193 visible around 22:19Z.
CME is associated with an eruption visible in SDO 171 behind the west limb.  The measurements are really rough since CME could only be visible in COR2 images and it was very faint.
The CME source was mostly back-sided, but there was sign of loop expansion and eruption from the AR right at the East limb in AIA coronal lines from 07:30-08:00Z.
The source was the eastern edge of the AR 2553 complex, an eruption with loops, dimming around N15E60, starting around 12:00Z.  The eruption can also be seen as an off-limb dimming in EUVI-A.
CME associated with an eruption visible in SDO 193 starting around 00UT.  The CME was very faint and only visible in the STA coronagraphs.
CME associated with a high latitude eruption visible in STA EUVI.  The CME could only be measured using POS approximations with STEREO_Cat.
CME associated with a far side eruption near disk center seen in STA EUVI images.
Faint CME associated with an eruption seen after 03:55 in STEREO A EUVI in the NE.
Source is eruption off the west limb. Opening field lines observed in AIA 193 around 22:36Z.
Eruption observed north of the equator off the west limb. Prominence seen in AIA 304 after 1:30Z.
Eruption off the south west limb. Opening magnetic field lines seen from AIA 304 after 6:15Z.

Starting around 03:30Z, there was an apparent eruption from the AR 2553-2558 complex located ~60 degrees west and spanning from ~5S to 15N.  There was some dimming and loop formation (best visible in AIA 193) and some off-limb loop motion (best visible in AIA 171).    However, these signatures may not be the full source of the CME, as they don't map well into the CME location.  There may be another loop in the CME with a different source.
Very faint CME associated with a N-S filament eruption west from AR2557 seen in AIA after 2016-06-22T1:20Z.
The active region in the middle of the STEREO A field of view. Can be seen via SDO/AIA 304 -- left limb.
Source can be seen in STEREO EUV north-west quadrant. Eruption takes place starting at about 2016-06-25T02:05Z.
CME associated with an eruption visible around N15E40 after a B5.8 class flare observed in SDO AIA.  CME is very narrow and somewhat difficult to measure since it is also faint.
Prominence eruption seen in the NW limb from SDO 131, 171, 193 and 304 at around 11:40 UT. Also can be seen from STEREO A EUVI 195 in the NE at around 11:25 UT.
Faint and narrow CME. Associated small backsided eruption is seen after 15:55 in EUVI A in the SE.  Seen in SW in C2.
CME associated with activity seen in an AR located at a longitude/latitude of about 165/10 degrees and visible in STA EUVI images.
CME associated with activity seen in an AR located around longitude/latitude 165/10 as observed in STA EUVI.  Small flares may be associated with the eruption from the same AR.
Source seen in STA EUV NE erupting at about 2016-06-30T15:45Z. Longitude may be potentially closer to -180 degrees.
Source location is unknown

No clear source was visible - there are some evolving loops above the NW limb visible in AIA 171 around 22:00Z, but it's not clear whether it was related to this CME or not.  No visible signature in EUVI-A.
This is associated with the long-duration B3.6 class flare peaked at 21:47Z.
The start time might not be accurate due to the CME being very faint.
eruption signature in AIA 193 very faint.
Source is below active region 2560. Eruption can be seen in SDO 304 starting around 12:50. Coronal dimming can be seen in SDO 193, near the west limb and below the active region 2560.
Slow dimming and field line evolution visible in AIA images from 06:30-08:30.

CME source could not be identified.  It can be seen that it corresponds to the far side of the Sun looking at the NE limb in SDO 171 where some movement of the field lines is somewhat visible.  Only POS measurements were made.
CME may be associated with the low M class flares originating from AR 2565 and 2567.  The measurements are very preliminary since the CME was very faint.

CME may be associated with the low M class flares originating from AR 2565 and 2567.  The measurements are very preliminary since the CME was faint and embedded in a previous one from the same source.

CME could possibly be from a filament eruption seen in STA EUVI at higher latitudes on the NW.  There is a coronal hole close to the eruption on the NW side.  CME was very faint and difficult to measure.

Source region located behind the west limb as seen from Earth. Opening field lines observed off the west limb in SDO AIA 171 and 193. Opening field lines and rising loops observed off Stereo A's east limb in EUVI 195.
large spray of cooler material ejected from the active region with this CME, associated with double peaked flare.
Source region is just beyond the west limb.
CME source is slightly South and just beyond the West limb from SDO, erupting with rising bright material and opening field lines around 04:37 and then again at 07:42.

The source of this CME is a filament eruption seen close to disk center in AIA 193/304 starting ~18:00 (with an activation/slow rise phase starting several hours earlier). AIA 304 shows the lift-off of the absorption structure, AIA 193 shows extended brightenings along the filament channel from about ~E20 to W40 in longitude. This eruption produced a faint halo in STA and C2/3.
Big eruption in the backside of the sun around N15E20 as seen on STA EUVI. CME was faint and the front was difficult to measure.

Small filament eruption across the lower hemisphere can be seen in SDO AIA193 seem to start around 2016-07-31T21:27Z.  Could be the source of CME, but it is not completely clear due to the timing. Significant dimming and movement of material can be seen above the eruption.

From a filament eruption in the NE quadrant. 

Halpha images indicate that a filament disappearance at ~17:30 (seen in H alpha NSO GONG
El Teide and H alpha GONG Big Bear) located in the NE quadrant
CME is associated with an eruption visible in STA EUVI close to center disk.  The CME was very faint and difficult to measure.  Eruption also caused some elevation in the lower energy protons in STA.

A small a very thin and faint CME can be seen in SOHO C2/3 starting at 03:34Z in C2. The source location is a small filament liftoff that can be seen just beyond the west limb in SDO 171 at 03:00Z.  CME was measured to have speeds around 550km/s
The source location can be seen as an eruption in STA EUVI 195 at 10:35Z in West limb.

2 eruptions seen in SXI and H alpha very close in time, 20:15 in AR 12571, and 20:28 at the west limb (AR 12570/72 complex, no SDO images available for these 2 events) - CME more likely from the second event at the west limb

There was a CME today starting around 02:00Z, as seen in C2. It is very slow moving and propagating out to the NW. It has yet to be measured because they are not enough frames in C3. Source is difficult to determine, but it may be associated with active region 2570, which is the source of last night's flare that began at 21:17Z.
Associated filament eruption seen in SDO 171 north-east starting at about 2016-08-10T00:50Z.

Eruption can be seen in Stereo A EUVI 195 at 15:15 off the far eastern limb.   Eruption caused strong opening field lines and dimming in the area.  Material can be seen moving up and away from the sun.  CME can be seen in STA EUVI starting at 17:24Z.

A filament lifted off the solar disk slightly east of center disk starting around 2016-08-12T13:00Z in SDO AIA 304 with dimmings seen in SDO 193.
Additional mass ejected off of NE limb seen in SDO AIA 171/304 at 18:00, which seems to be triggered by the on-disk eruption at ~13:00. 
A very slow moving mass can be seen coming out in C2 starting at around 15:00Z, but by the time it reaches C3 it looks more like slow moving outflow.
source region is at the NE limb in STA, in EUVI opening of field lines can be seen starting ~2016-08-12T12:25Z.

There was a large eruption on the northern pole towards the eastern limb. Can be seen as a dimming and breaking/opening field lines in SDO AIA 304 and 171. The aftermath of the eruption can be seen in STA.
CME associated with a very slow eruption from an un-numbered AR on the NE seen in SDO and STA EUVI.  The CME front was very difficult to measure (very faint).

source located on far side, eruption can be seen in EUVI A 195 in the NW, filament starting to lift off at ~16:15 close to west limb, dimmings and post-eruptive brightenings visble forming a S-shaped structure. AIA 193 shows opening of field line off limb in the NE and post-eruptive arcade loops visible above the limb at ~18.00. AIA 304 also shows the lift off of the filament at the east limb.
Source could not be located and start time is following the difference images.  The CME is very slow and narrow.
The CME was associated with a filament eruption observed in the north-west hemisphere (AIA304)
A small prominence eruption observed at the limb of the Sun in SDO AIA 304 from 01:00-03:00Z.
AIA data were not available, but there appeared to be some evolution (brightening, minor dimming) from an AR near the NW limb in STEREO EUVI-A 195 images starting around 07:00Z

Source region of this CME could not be identified. Considering that it moves to the SW in C2/3, the lack of any other source and a possible longitude of +60 or +120 from SWPC_cat, the CME probably originated in AR 12580. Hardly any SDO images are available (due to JSOC problems) and SWAP images do not show any signs of eruption though.
Start time is time when seen in SOHO LASCO C3 due to a data gap in SOHO LASCO C2 imagery.

A very slow and faint CME can be seen coming out in the western limb of C2 starting at 12:12Z.  The source location is unclear in the imagery but it is believed to be farsided because a clear eruption is not seen in SDO or the visible part of STA EUVI. Speed found to be around 108 km/s.
The source is the dimming visible around S15W15 in SDO AIA 193 starting at 07:42Z and continuing with rising loops and more dimming to the North.

The CME was very hard to measure due to its faintness and location. The source appears to be the active region on the backside of the Sun (N15E50 as seen by STA). Eruption can be seen by SDO 171 as beyond the west limb around 05:15Z.

Opening field lines and rising loops visible in AIA 193, AIA 171, and EUVI 195. CME is directed to the southwest as seen from Earth. Eruption appears near the limb as seen from both Earth and Stereo A.
The eruption comes from the west limb.  Starting at around 07:22 in SDO 171, field lines can be seen opening.

Source is an eruption in the SW of STA EUVI imagery starting around 22:35 with some dimming and post eruption arcades visible. Corresponding opening field lines seen on the SE limb in SDO 171 and 193 around 23:00.
Possible source may be the west limb as seen by STA.  Around 11:25 in STA EUVI imagery there seem to be some opening and closing field lines.
This CME is associated with a filament eruption seen around 04:13Z in the northeast of SDO AIA 193.
Can see it distinctly in STA Cor2.

This slow moving CME can be seen in both the STA and C2 imagery.  Its source location is unclear, but is most likely coming from a faint eruption that can be seen in the STA imagery at ~05:00Z on the far western limb.
Starting at 10:12, there was a rising loop on the NE limb of SDO AIA 171, which likely caused a narrow CME starting at 11:12 in the NE of C2 that is also faintly visible in NW of STA Cor2.
This CME is associated with a field line eruption and material lifting off in S30E50 of SDO 171 around 14:00Z.
This CME is associated with a far sided eruption characterized by dimming and rising loops. It is seen in the NE of STA EUVI imagery around 2016-09-22T23:30Z.
A leading edge was difficult to determine and the CME was not visible in C3, so the speed may be artificially high.
Measurement was done only using STA as the CME appeared mainly to the SW in both coronagraphs, making it difficult to fit to both, while C3 data was very hazy.
CME is very narrow and has no anticipated space weather consequences. The source is a filament eruption near the west limb, visible in SDO 304 starting at 2016-09-27T00:13Z.

An eruption can be seen in S28W50 at 09-27T22:51Z in SDO/AIA 304, 193 and 171 with brightening.  There was also a filament eruption in SW region at 20:45Z in SDO/AIA 304, 193 and 171.
The CME is from a combination of these two events.  A narrow portion of the CME can be seen, which comes from the brightening, while the wider part is from the filament.
Able to observe field lines lifting off in S14W80(AR2597) of SDO 171 & 193 starting around 17:00Z.

CME was caused by a large filament eruption that can be seen just NE of center disk starting at 2016-10-01T00:30Z.  Lots of twisting field lines and rising loops can be seen as the filament lifts off in addition to significant dimming in the region afterwords.

Its source is some dimming, post-eruptive loops, and brightening at around 10-01T14:36Z, seen in SDO AIA 193 to the east where the earlier large filament had erupted.  Can also see footprint brightening in 304.

CME is caused by a large eruption that can be seen from a small filament lift off on the western limb in STA starting at 12:55Z.   The eruption can also be seen in SDO on the western limb at ~13:00Z with large moving and opening field lines.
SDO AIA 171 shows rising loops, opening field lines, and outflowing material beginning to pick up around 02:42 off the SW limb, which is likely the source for this CME.
Source was a filament eruption off the western limb around 07:00, visible in SDO 171 and 304.
Source is not entirely certain but appears to be related to some faint movement of field lines around AR2600, visible in SDO 171 at about 06:00.

Halo CME seen in C2 at 10-09T02:36Z and C3 at 10-09T06:30Z.  Visible to the NW as seen by STA at 10-08T21:09Z (but there is a data gap from 10-09T16:39Z-21:09).  Source is a filament eruption starting around 2016-10-08T15:00Z in the NE, visible in SDO 304.

There was a brightening and possible dimming seen just south of disk center on the far side in STA EUVI imagery around 01:55Z. Also, there is a brightening along the southern polar coronal hole boundary that may be associated with a large-scale coronal configuration.
Source region unclear. May be a surge visible off the East limb in SDO AIA 193 difference imaging. Faint rising loops can be seen after a data gap ending at 2016-10-11T02:05 in EUVI A.
The source is a very small eruption slightly north of AR2599, visible in SDO 193 around 16:00. The eruption is characterized by a brief but clearly visible brightening in the region.
Between 04:00-06:00 there is some movement in the field lines in the NE of SDO AIA 171, and corresponding opening field loop in the NW of STA EUVI imagery that is likely the source of the CME.
CME is associated with dimming seen around 10:05Z in the SW of STA EUVI imagery, as well as opening field lines and movement seen off the southeast limb in SDO AIA 193.

The source is an eruption off the SE limb visible in SDO 171 around 14:46 characterized by rapid movement of field lines in the region. This is also visible off the SW limb in STA EUVI around the same time.
Source is a static filament that can be seen lifting off starting at around 20:00Z on the 16th directly above the western limb.  The eruption is very slow.
The source is the significant dimming followed by post-eruptive arcades beginning at 20:55 on 2016-10-17 and centered around N20W20 as visible in SDO AIA 193.
Source is a filament eruption around 13:00 visible in the NW of SDO 193 and 304
Source is an eruption that was characterized by strong moving field lines and post arcade loops.  A large burst of plasma can be seen in AIA 304, but most of the plasma fell back into the sun.

The source is the opening field lines that can be seen moving off the NW limb starting around 10:24Z in SDO AIA 193. Measured using only C2 because it wasn't visible in STA and the stick in the C3 coronagraph blocked the path of the CME.

The source is very difficult to determine. The only activity that may correspond are some very faint opening field lines visible in SDO AIA 171 off the SE limb around 19:15Z, but this could be too late to be the source. Measurements are from a few frames in C2, better parameters can be obtained when more data comes in.
CME came form an eruption that can be seen in SDO EUVI 195 in the NE of around 22:00.
So far only measured based on C2. Most likely associated with limb ejection observed in AIA 171 at 20:22.
The source is difficult to determine but could be the darkening seen in SDO AIA 193 around S40W60 beginning around 2016-10-24T22:00Z
CME is associated with a large eruption on the backside visible in STA EUVI around 18:30. Resulting movement of field lines can be seen off the SE limb in SDO 171 afterwards.
Source is unclear.
CME visible in the SW of STA with likely source an eruption in the SE of SDO AIA 193 at 05:00UT.
Source is large filament eruption off the NW limb visible in SDO 171 around 09:30, and earlier in SDO 304.
This CME is associated with movement of field lines in the west around 07:00 in SDO AIA 193.
Source is likely large eruption seen in SDO AIA in the West limb starting at ~23:00Z coming from AR 2603.
Not visible in C2/C3 so measured only with STA. Source is very faint filament seen in the SW of SDO AIA 304 that begins to rise around 07:00Z on 10-29 and lifts off around 15:00Z on 10-29.

Measured using only C2 because it was too faint to appear in C3 & not visible in STA. The source is likely the dimming visible near a small coronal hole in AIA 193 around S30E30 starting around 10:43Z
Source is rising loops seen on the limb in SDO AIA 193 and STA EUVI imagery around 23:15Z.
Source is 12:55 filament eruption visible at the SE limb of STA EUVI 195.
Source is a filament eruption at 03:00 in SDO AIA 193 around S30W30. Filament can be seen leaving the disk, followed by dimming.

This CME is not visible in C2 and C3. A possible source is an eruption near the east limb in STA EUVI imagery, but there is a data gap from 2016-11-04T15:45Z to 2016-11-04T21:45Z, so it is difficult to tell.
This partial halo CME is associated with a filament eruption and dimming starting at 03:00Z to the NW of disk center in SDO AIA 193.
Source is unclear: it is likely a back-sided eruption in the AR visible in STA EUVI near the western limb. The eruption likely occurred during the data gap in STA from 16:15 to 19:25.
The source is a large eruption visible in 193, 304, 171 and 131 around N15E50 at 00:46Z. In 193, the eruption is characterized by a rapid ejection of material across a small surface area.
The source is unclear, no specific AR seems to be displaying signs of an eruption in this region other than the AR which caused the CME with start time 01:25.
The source is thought to be the back-sided eruption around 2016-11-09T04:00Z, visible in STA EUVI at S15E45 (from STA perspective) and characterized by rapid dimming in the AR located in this region.
Source is the far-sided filament that occurred at 13:55 in the the NE limb as seen by STA.
Starting around 08:15 there are some rising loops/opening field lines off the SE limb of SDO AIA 171 and 193.
source location is behind the limb for both SDO and EUVI A, some indication for an eruption in EUVI behind the east limb with field lines rising off limb.
Source is unclear but believed to be the AR seen in the NE of STA EUVI 195.
